raw:
{
  "weights": {"R1.1": 0.3, "R1.2": 0.3, "R1.3": 0.3, "R1.4": 0.0, "R1.5": 0.7, "R1.6": 1.0, "R1.7": 0.0, "RX.1": 1.0, "RX.2": 1.0, "RX.3": 1.0, "RX.4": 1.0, "RX.5": 1.0},
  "active_set": ["R1.5", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What scope of improvement should I target? A) Light copyedit and wording-only polish (no structural or content changes), B) Moderate rewrite to reorganize sections/subsections and tighten narrative while preserving core claims/methods, or C) Substantive revision that also reframes the contribution and argument and checks claim–evidence alignment (no full literature audit unless requested).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.5": "Improving the draft likely benefits from refining section/subsection ordering and narrative flow to make the contribution and methods easier to follow.",
    "R1.6": "The request to improve the draft centrally calls for sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and style polish.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should transparently preserve or improve statements about uncertainty and limitations without introducing unjustified certainty.",
    "RX.2": "Rewording must avoid amplifying claims beyond what the reported results and ablations support.",
    "RX.3": "The work should remain within the scope of improving the provided text rather than adding new claims, experiments, or citations.",
    "RX.4": "Language about abstention and deployment should not be revised in ways that could encourage unsafe or misleading use.",
    "RX.5": "Any rephrasing must keep reported metrics, model details, and dataset descriptions traceable to the original text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What scope of improvement should I target? A) Light copyedit and wording-only polish (no structural or content changes), B) Moderate rewrite to reorganize sections/subsections and tighten narrative while preserving core claims/methods, or C) Substantive revision that also reframes the contribution and argument and checks claim–evidence alignment (no full literature audit unless requested).

rationale.R1.5: Improving the draft likely benefits from refining section/subsection ordering and narrative flow to make the contribution and methods easier to follow.
rationale.R1.6: The request to improve the draft centrally calls for sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and style polish.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should transparently preserve or improve statements about uncertainty and limitations without introducing unjustified certainty.
rationale.RX.2: Rewording must avoid amplifying claims beyond what the reported results and ablations support.
rationale.RX.3: The work should remain within the scope of improving the provided text rather than adding new claims, experiments, or citations.
rationale.RX.4: Language about abstention and deployment should not be revised in ways that could encourage unsafe or misleading use.
rationale.RX.5: Any rephrasing must keep reported metrics, model details, and dataset descriptions traceable to the original text.
